prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Kohn–Sham HOMO, LUMO energies and the orbital (Koopmans-style) gap of aniline; headline quantity = HOMO–LUMO gap (eV).
Level of theory: DFT tier “standard” = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density fitting (PySCF backend).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (14-atom aniline) from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no re-optimization – this is a single-point DFT calculation on that fixed geometry (DFT//input xyz).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell RKS).
Software: PySCF (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 9 cycles; no engine warnings reported. Density-fitting (RI/JK) was used, an auxiliary-basis approximation to the two-electron integrals.
Live output & files: the run streamed its engine log to a live .out file announced at launch; structured results are in /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpxd6wwhsb/live.json (see cli_invocation). Re-running: chemkit frontier-orbitals --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 aniline.xyz --out <path>.json

Results (gas-phase, B3LYP/def2-TZVP):
• Total electronic energy = –287.71578 Hartree (–7829.1453 eV)
• HOMO energy  (index 24) = –5.553772 eV
• LUMO energy  (index 25) = –0.2489544 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV (headline value reported).

No warnings were returned by chemkit.

The calculation is fully converged and passes chemkit’s integrity checks (integrity.trustworthy: true).
